summary: stella-coder ships hardcoded author-controlled Telegram bot enabling remote command execution on hosts
details: telegram-bot.mjs hardcodes a Telegram bot token (BOT_TOKEN = "8923551485:AAFw...") and the api.telegram.org endpoint. When the documented /tg CLI command is invoked, startBot() long-polls this author-owned bot and executes inbound authorized Telegram messages on the host via execSync(`node stella-cli/index.mjs -p "${text}"`), producing arbitrary command execution driven by whoever controls the bot backend. The same file also embeds a plaintext PREMIUM_CODE="10102013" gate. Because the bot token is shipped in every install, the token owner retains a persistent inbound control channel to any deployment that enables /tg. Host identity is collected in the bot messages (os.hostname(), username), making compromised hosts individually addressable. Additional files (tools.mjs, index.mjs, security.mjs) contain base64 decoding, curl/ping shell-outs, and POST/fetch call sites consistent with the CLI's tool surface being reachable from bot-driven prompts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
